robots.txt allows the public guide URL for generic crawlers with a crawl delay; blocked bot user agents were not used.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery for Canadian University Dubai found one official CUD Learning Resource Centre page with specific guidance on citing generative AI. Official repository PDFs and student handbook/catalog results were documented but rejected for this crawl because they were research/program/handbook context rather than source text governing student or staff generative AI use.

artifacts.sourceTitle: CUD Learning Resource Centre: APA, IEEE & AMA citation styles: Citing Artificial Intelligence (AI)

artifacts.claimText: Canadian University Dubai's Learning Resource Centre guide includes guidance that generative AI tools should be cited when AI-created content is paraphrased, quoted, or incorporated into a user's work, and that functional uses such as editing or translation should be acknowledged.

citation.citationTitle: CUD Learning Resource Centre: APA, IEEE & AMA citation styles: Citing Artificial Intelligence (AI)
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: CUD Learning Resource Centre: APA, IEEE & AMA citation styles: Citing Artificial Intelligence (AI)
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The Modern Language Association (MLA) recommendations for citing generative AI are the following; cite a generative AI tool whenever you paraphrase, quote, or incorporate into your own work any content (whether text, image, data, or other) that was created by it; acknowledge all functional uses of the tool (like editing your prose or translating words) in a note, your text, or another suitable location; take care to vet the secondary sources it cites.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: CUD's Learning Resource Centre guide presents MLA recommendations to cite generative AI when using AI-created content and to acknowledge functional uses such as editing or translation.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow library-guidance claim only. The evidence does not support a binding university-wide AI policy claim.

artifacts.title: Canadian University Dubai AI citation guidance source discovery
artifacts.summary: The staged crawl found official CUD Learning Resource Centre guidance about citing generative AI. It did not find enough official evidence for a binding university-wide generative AI policy, approved-tool, privacy, security, or assessment-rule claim.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The approved claim is scoped to CUD Learning Resource Centre citation guidance and should not be read as a binding university-wide generative AI policy.
